reforms.description: The Finnish government initiated a reform to extend minimum compulsory schooling to 9 years and is implementing a rapid expansion of tertiary education to increase the academic qualifications of the population, reflecting a significant shift in educational policy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The expansion of education is expected to enhance the skills and qualifications of the workforce, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been announced but not yet fully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a structural change in the education system aimed at improving human capital.
reforms.source_quote: Extension of minimum compulsory schooling to 9 years under an ongoing reform of the basic education system initiated in 1972 and due to be completed by 1978.
